steps: Go to https://dashboard.plaid.com/signup and create a free developer account
Verify your email address and complete the account setup
Log in to the Plaid Dashboard at https://dashboard.plaid.com/developers/api
Under 'Team Settings', click 'Keys' to view your credentials
Copy your 'client_id' — this is used as the PLAID-CLIENT-ID header value
Copy the 'Sandbox secret' for sandbox testing, or 'Development'/'Production' secret for the corresponding environment
Note: Plaid uses two values together — a client_id and a secret — sent in the request body (not a single API key header)

credential_fields.name: api_key
credential_fields.label: Client ID
credential_fields.description: Your Plaid client_id, sent as the PLAID-CLIENT-ID header

notes: Plaid requires both a client_id and a secret in most API request bodies — the manifest maps to client_id as the primary key
Sandbox environment is free and available immediately; Development and Production require approval
The secret is environment-specific: sandbox, development, and production secrets are separate
Apply for Production access via the Dashboard after testing in Sandbox
All Plaid API endpoints require POST with JSON body including client_id and secret fields

test_endpoint.description: Retrieve a paginated list of financial institutions (no linked user required)
